penalize.name: animal-only
penalize.reason: Pure animal/ex-vivo study without direct clinical linkage

penalize.name: molecular-only
penalize.reason: Pure molecular/mechanistic study without clinical context

pubmed_query: "ventilator-induced lung injury"[Title/Abstract] OR "ventilator induced lung injury"[Title/Abstract] OR "ventilator-associated lung injury"[Title/Abstract] OR "ventilator associated lung injury"[Title/Abstract] OR "mechanical ventilation-induced lung injury"[Title/Abstract] OR "Ventilator-Induced Lung Injury"[MeSH Terms] OR "lung protective ventilation"[Title/Abstract] OR "lung-protective ventilation"[Title/Abstract] OR ("driving pressure"[Title/Abstract] AND ARDS[Title/Abstract]) OR ("mechanical power"[Title/Abstract] AND (lung[Title/Abstract] OR ventilation[Title/Abstract])) OR (VILI[Title/Abstract] AND (lung[Title/Abstract] OR ventilation[Title/Abstract] OR ARDS[Title/Abstract])) OR (VALI[Title/Abstract] AND (lung[Title/Abstract] OR ventilation[Title/Abstract] OR ARDS[Title/Abstract]))

expected_papers.title: Ventilator-induced lung injury.
expected_papers.reason: NEJM 2013 Slutsky & Ranieri — canonical VILI review

expected_papers.title: Ventilator-induced lung injury: lessons from experimental studies.
expected_papers.reason: AJRCCM 1998 Dreyfuss & Saumon — foundational VILI mechanisms

expected_papers.title: Ventilation with lower tidal volumes as compared with traditional tidal volumes for acute lung injury and the acute respiratory distress syndrome.
expected_papers.reason: NEJM 2000 ARDSNet — landmark trial defining low-tidal-volume ventilation

expected_papers.title: Driving Pressure and Survival in the Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome.
expected_papers.reason: NEJM 2015 Amato — driving pressure as the central VILI mortality predictor

expected_papers.title: Ventilator-related causes of lung injury: the mechanical power.
expected_papers.reason: Intensive Care Medicine 2016 Gattinoni — mechanical power concept

expected_papers.title: Mechanical power of ventilation is associated with mortality in critically ill patients: an analysis of patients in two observational cohorts.
expected_papers.reason: Intensive Care Medicine 2018 Serpa Neto — mechanical power × mortality

expected_papers.title: Prone positioning in severe acute respiratory distress syndrome.
expected_papers.reason: NEJM 2013 PROSEVA — landmark RCT for severe ARDS / VILI prevention

gap_subtopics.name: Mechanical power
gap_subtopics.gap_statement: No mechanical-power evidence was identified in the accepted set.
gap_subtopics.why_it_matters: Mechanical power integrates rate, volume, and pressure into a single VILI risk metric increasingly used at the bedside.
gap_subtopics.best_design: Multicentre observational cohort or RCT with mechanical-power titration

gap_subtopics.name: Driving pressure
gap_subtopics.gap_statement: No driving-pressure evidence was found in the accepted set.
gap_subtopics.why_it_matters: Driving pressure is the single ventilator parameter most strongly associated with mortality in ARDS (Amato 2015).
gap_subtopics.best_design: Pooled RCT analysis or driving-pressure-targeted RCT

gap_subtopics.name: Prone positioning
gap_subtopics.gap_statement: No prone-positioning evidence in the accepted set.
gap_subtopics.why_it_matters: Prone positioning reduces VILI by homogenizing ventilation; standard of care in severe ARDS.
gap_subtopics.best_design: Implementation study or pragmatic trial in non-ARDS hypoxemic respiratory failure

gap_subtopics.name: P-SILI / spontaneous breathing injury
gap_subtopics.gap_statement: No P-SILI / spontaneous-breathing injury evidence in the accepted set.
gap_subtopics.why_it_matters: High-effort spontaneous breathing during respiratory failure can drive lung injury independent of the ventilator.
gap_subtopics.best_design: Prospective cohort with esophageal pressure monitoring

gap_subtopics.name: Pediatric / neonatal VILI
gap_subtopics.gap_statement: No pediatric or neonatal VILI evidence in the accepted set.
gap_subtopics.why_it_matters: Pediatric and neonatal lungs respond differently to mechanical ventilation; PARDS guidelines diverge from adult.
gap_subtopics.best_design: Pediatric ARDS network cohort or PARDS-specific trial

gap_subtopics.name: Lung rest on ECMO
gap_subtopics.gap_statement: No ECMO-related VILI / lung-rest evidence in the accepted set.
gap_subtopics.why_it_matters: On ECMO, ventilation can be ultra-protective; optimal rest settings remain debated.
gap_subtopics.best_design: ECMO registry analysis or trial comparing rest strategies
